title: When should you give up on the niche and take an L?

transcript.text: How many months would you give a YouTube niche before you give up and quit? >> two types [music] of niches, how I see it. Simple niches, like AI, whiteboard, commentary. Those blow up fast and then die out pretty quickly, too. Typically, all of this happens in 3 to 4 months. So, if it doesn't work from [music] the first seven videos, you are already too late and the cost of failure is nothing, so just analyze why it failed [music] and move on to another niche. Complicated branded niches, on the other hand, take longer to blow up and they are way more sustainable. [music] So, if this niche doesn't work somewhere here, then first figure out and try to understand whether it's a content issue or channel issue. For example, if you're getting zero views, it's the channel issue, simply relaunch on another channel with the same content. If you're getting couple thousand views, then most likely it's the content [music] issue and you should be optimizing for better packaging, better titles, hooks, and the storytelling structure.
